fact: May 19 is exactly 139 days into the year - that's almost exactly 38% of the whole year done!
In the Northern Hemisphere, this is prime time for baby birds to be learning how to fly, so you might see lots of clumsy crash landings in your yard.
The sun rises before 6 AM in most northern places by this date, making it one of the earliest sunrise times of the year.
May 19 falls smack in the middle of Taurus season - the zodiac sign of the stubborn bull that loves food and naps.
Dandelions are going absolutely crazy right now, shooting their fluffy seed parachutes everywhere like nature's confetti cannons.
In Australia and other Southern Hemisphere places, this date marks the heart of autumn when leaves are turning brilliant orange and red.
The moon phase on May 19 changes every year, but when it's a full moon on this date, it's called the Flower Moon because everything is blooming like mad.
This date sits exactly 33 days after the spring equinox in the north, when day and night were perfectly equal.
Mosquitoes are just starting to wake up and get really annoying around this time in many places - thanks, warm weather!
Tree pollen is at its absolute worst around May 19 in many regions, turning cars yellow and making people sneeze like crazy.
The sun sets after 7:30 PM in most northern cities by this date, giving kids extra daylight for playing outside after dinner.
May 19 is only about 32 days away from the summer solstice - the longest day of the year in the north!
